comments.body: AC1 credential-verified (2026-07-11): the rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS email subscription to joeoakhart@navapbc.com is CONFIRMED and delivering — get-subscription-attributes returns PendingConfirmation=false (SubscriptionArn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:896586841071:rebar-gerrit-alerts:1ce0a8bd-b7ac-4256-bea6-87889af05993). The pending-confirmation risk that spawned this ticket is resolved; AC1 is met without needing a confirmation-free replacement sink.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pre-merge terraform plan (ReadOnlyAccess creds, live AWS) = 'Plan: 4 to add, 1 to change, 0 to destroy'. The 4 adds are exactly this ticket's resources (gerrit_data_disk_high alarm + gha_terraform_plan role/policy/attachment) and apply cleanly. The '1 to change' is PRE-EXISTING drift NOT authored here: aws_instance.gerrit user_data 676ec059->1cfc59bd (committed HCL vs live instance) — a genuine dc3f-class un-applied drift that the new terraform-drift CI (AC2b) would correctly flag. CONSEQUENCE: the post-merge apply of THIS ticket's resources must be TARGETED (terraform apply -target=aws_iam_role.gha_terraform_plan -target=... -target=aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.gerrit_data_disk_high) so it does NOT sweep in the unrelated instance user_data change. That instance drift is separate work to triage.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASSED (round 2, 0 blocking) + signed certified plan-review attestation. Advisory triage (14 findings, all non-blocking; tradeoffs accepted): (1) ReadOnlyAccess is broader than a hand-rolled describe/list policy — ACCEPTED: it's a plan-only role (no write/apply), and enumerating every describe/list action across EC2/SNS/CloudWatch/SSM/IAM/DLM/S3/EBS would be a brittle, drift-prone inline policy; ReadOnlyAccess + kms:Decrypt-via-SSM is the pragmatic least-privilege for a read-only plan. (2) repo:navapbc/rebar:* sub matches any branch/PR — ACCEPTED for a read-only role (worst case: a plan runs); can narrow to a specific ref later if desired. (3) -lock=false bypasses S3 state locking — INTENTIONAL: a read-only plan needs no lock and this avoids granting S3 PutObject. (4) SUBSTANTIVE sequencing note: the terraform-drift.yml workflow lands before rebar-terraform-plan exists in AWS, so the FIRST CI run fails until the post-merge apply creates the role — mitigation: apply AC2a promptly after merge (documented in the ticket's post-merge operator step); the check going green is itself the confirmation the role is live. (5) Title predates AC3 — cosmetic. (6) runbook/retire-path doc updates — minor follow-ups, out of scope for this change.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Three follow-ups spun out of dc3f (puzzling-obsidian-bunny) after its core gap — 3 undeployed
CloudWatch alarms — was fixed by a terraform apply: (1) confirm the alert SNS subscription
actually delivers, (2) add terraform drift-detection CI so committed-but-unapplied HCL cannot
sit unnoticed (the root cause of dc3f), and (3) bring a live-but-unmanaged alarm under IaC.

These are bundled deliberately: all three harden the SAME alerting/IaC chain for the rebar-gerrit
host and are small. Each AC below names a single unambiguous disposition (no open design choice
left to the executor).

## Scope

- rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS email subscription (the alert sink).
- No terraform CI exists (apply is manual per infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md), so committed
  HCL can silently sit unapplied — the dc3f root cause.
- One live alarm (rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high) exists in AWS but is absent from HCL/state and has
  an EMPTY alarm_actions list (never notifies).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] [operator-attested] AC1 — SNS subscription delivers. The rebar-gerrit-alerts email
  subscription to joeoakhart@navapbc.com is CONFIRMED (not PendingConfirmation) so alarms
  actually deliver. Evidence: a ticket comment recording the get-subscription-attributes result
  (PendingConfirmation=false) with the SubscriptionArn and date. NOTE: verified 2026-07-11 —
  PendingConfirmation=false; this AC is already met and needs NO destructive replacement sink.
- [ ] AC2a — OIDC role (least-privilege, plan-only). The GitHub OIDC provider
  (token.actions.githubusercontent.com) ALREADY EXISTS in account 896586841071 (verified); this
  ticket adds a NEW IAM role `rebar-terraform-plan` in infra/terraform/iam.tf whose trust policy
  is scoped to `repo:navapbc/rebar:*` (sub condition) with aud=sts.amazonaws.com, and whose
  permissions are AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess + one inline statement granting kms:Decrypt
  constrained by kms:ViaService=ssm (for the alert-endpoint SecureString data source). No apply
  or write permissions — plan-only, least privilege.
- [ ] AC2b — drift-detection CI. `.github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml` runs
  `terraform plan -input=false -lock=false -detailed-exitcode` in infra/terraform, authenticating
  via GitHub OIDC to `rebar-terraform-plan`. `-detailed-exitcode` makes a non-empty plan (exit 2)
  FAIL the check. Triggers: pull_request touching infra/terraform/**, push to main touching
  infra/terraform/** (catches already-committed-but-unapplied drift, not just PR diffs), a daily
  schedule, and workflow_dispatch.
- [ ] AC3 — codify + import the orphan alarm (keep coverage; non-destructive). Add
  `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.gerrit_data_disk_high` (name rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high;
  namespace rebar/host, metric disk_used_percent published by observability.sh, dims
  InstanceId=data.aws_instance.gerrit.id + mount=/var/gerrit, threshold 85, GTE) to
  infra/terraform/monitoring.tf WITH alarm_actions/ok_actions = the SNS topic (closing the
  silent-alarm gap), then `terraform import` it into state so HCL/state/reality agree. The alarm
  is KEPT (data-volume disk pressure is real coverage), not deleted.

## Verification

- AC1: `aws sns get-subscription-attributes` shows PendingConfirmation=false (recorded as a
  comment).
- AC2/AC3: `terraform validate` passes and `terraform plan` (ReadOnlyAccess creds) shows the new
  role/policies/alarm apply cleanly with no errors — run pre-merge in the worktree.
- Post-merge operator step (needs live creds): `terraform import
  aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.gerrit_data_disk_high rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high` then
  `terraform apply` to wire the alarm actions and create the role; the terraform-drift CI then
  reports green (no drift) on the next run.

## Notes / decisions (resolve prior plan-review BLOCKs)

- Shared S3 state backend (rebar-tfstate-896586841071) means terraform writes (import/apply) MUST
  follow the HCL merge — importing to shared state before merge would make an un-branched plan
  read the alarm as "to destroy". So this ticket lands the reviewed HCL/CI via Gerrit; the
  import/apply is the documented post-merge operator step above.
- AC1's original "OR replace with a confirmation-free sink" option is dropped: the subscription is
  already confirmed, so no destructive substitution (which risked dark alerting) is needed.
compiled_state.title: rebar-gerrit alerting hardening: confirm/replace SNS subscription + add terraform drift CI

verify_commands.dd_text: HCL is syntactically valid and internally consistent
the ticket resources plan cleanly against live AWS
